name: Helsinki
summary: Helsinki is Finland's seaside capital, nicknamed 'Daughter of the Baltic' because it sits on a peninsula sprinkled with hundreds of little islands. You can ride a ferry to a real sea fortress, find Moomin trolls in shops, and warm up like a Finn in a sauna. In summer the sun barely sets, so the sky stays bright almost all night long.
funFacts: The Moomins, those round white hippo-shaped trolls, were invented in Helsinki by Finnish artist Tove Jansson in 1945 as a cheerful escape from wartime gloom.
Suomenlinna is a giant sea fortress spread across six rocky islands and was named a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1991 for its old cannons and tunnels.
Finland has around 3.2 million saunas for only 5.5 million people, so there is roughly one steamy sauna for every two Finns, including babies.
In June and July the sun in Helsinki barely dips below the horizon, giving almost 19 hours of daylight and glowing 'white nights' instead of darkness.
Finland is home to 188,000 lakes and the official hometown of Santa Claus, who lives way up north in Lapland and answers letters from kids worldwide.
thingsToDo: Hop on the 15-minute HSL ferry from Market Square out to Suomenlinna sea fortress and follow the Blue Route past tunnels, cannons and the King's Gate.
Stand in Senate Square and climb the wide steps of Helsinki Cathedral, the white church with five green domes that watches over the whole city.
Step inside Temppeliaukio Rock Church, a round chapel carved straight into solid bedrock in 1969, and listen to how the rough stone walls echo.
Slurp creamy salmon soup or a warm cinnamon bun at the Old Market Hall (Vanha Kauppahalli), Helsinki's oldest food hall, opened on the harbour in 1889.
Ride the 1951 wooden Vuoristorata roller coaster and the giant Taiga coaster at Linnanmäki, Finland's oldest amusement park, with eight coasters and 42 rides total.
Hunt for Moomintroll, Snufkin and Little My at the Moomin Shop on Esplanadi, picking out a plush troll souvenir before walking back to the ship.

localWord.meaning: a friendly, casual way to say 'hi' in Finnish

images.alt: The big white Helsinki Cathedral with green domes stands tall above Senate Square on a sunny day.
A bird's-eye view of Suomenlinna sea fortress, a chain of green islands surrounded by blue Baltic water.
Inside the Rock Church, a round room carved into stone with a shiny copper ceiling overhead.
Helsinki's red-brick Old Market Hall by the harbour, where people buy yummy Finnish treats.
A green Helsinki tram glides along the street on a sunny summer evening.
